summary: Iteration 12 (frontend-only) — validated the new GR Meter feature in SelectedChannelPanel. All 15 sub-tests passed: element exists with data-testid='sel-gr-meter', 24 segments rendered, GR/dB labels visible, db readout = '—' when comp+lim both disabled (color rgb(68,68,68) = #444), readout = '0.0' in yellow rgb(255,214,10) = #FFD60A when COMP toggled ON (reduction is 0 in headless because AudioContext is suspended without user gesture/signal — expected per problem statement), readout remains active when only LIM enabled, returns to '—' when both disabled. Regression checks all green: 6 Comp pills (Thr/Ratio/Att/Rel/Knee/Mk) visible, sel-fader visible, EQ modal opens, channel switch to pill-out_phy_1 keeps GR meter functional, sel-close button works.

issues: Recharts warning 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' fires twice when the EQ modal opens — pre-existing console warning, not introduced by this iteration. Cosmetic, no visual impact.

critical_code_review_comments: GRMeter.jsx is clean: RAF-driven polling at ~30fps with peak-hold + slow decay implemented correctly; correctly stops reading when enabled=false (returns 0). Color thresholds (yellow<0.45, orange 0.45-0.75, red>0.75 of segment ratio) match the spec.
audioEngine.getCompReduction handles both Number and AudioParam-style .reduction property — good cross-browser shim.
Minor: data-testid for the meter bar container itself would help future automation verify individual lit segments; only sel-gr-meter (root) and sel-gr-meter-db (readout) are exposed. Not blocking.
Column 2 width widened to 260px to fit CompCurve(200) + GRMeter(20) + gap — layout still fits cleanly within the 3-column grid.
context_for_next_testing_agent: GR Meter feature fully verified in iteration 12. Reduction value stays at 0.0 in headless tests because AudioContext is suspended (no user-gesture audio playback) — that's expected and documented. To verify actual meter movement under signal, a manual test with audio routed through an enabled comp/limiter is required. All prior iteration regressions (1-11) remain intact.
